steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank completes an indirect (embedded) question: “Moskowitz was hired … to determine how much artificial sweetener ______.” Indirect questions must use statement word order (subject before verb) and end with a period, not a question mark.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'do most people prefer in a diet drink?': inverts the subject and auxiliary verb (“do most people”), which is direct-question word order. An embedded question requires normal declarative order. Also ends with a question mark, incorrect for a statement. ✗
  • Choice B — 'do most people prefer in a diet drink.': same word-order error as A (“do most people prefer” is direct-question syntax), making the sentence ungrammatical. ✗
  • Choice C — 'most people prefer in a diet drink?': word order is correct (subject “most people” before verb “prefer”), but ends with a question mark. The sentence as a whole is a statement, so it must end with a period. ✗
  • Choice D — 'most people prefer in a diet drink.': uses correct declarative word order and ends with a period, properly closing the statement. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
